title: Voter Eligibility
simpleExplanation: The basic requirements a person must meet to be legally allowed to vote.
whyItMatters: It ensures that only legal citizens who have reached adulthood participate in deciding the country's future.
whatVoterShouldKnow: You must be an Indian citizen, 18 years or older on the qualifying date, and an ordinary resident of your polling area.
beginnerNote: Just turning 18 is not enough. You must also formally apply to get your name on the voter list.

title: Voter Registration
simpleExplanation: The process of officially applying to be added to the electoral roll.
whyItMatters: Without registration, you cannot vote even if you meet all eligibility criteria.
whatVoterShouldKnow: Form 6 is used for new voter registration. It can be filled online or offline.
beginnerNote: Keep your age and address proof ready when applying.

title: Voter List / Electoral Roll
simpleExplanation: The official list of all registered voters in a specific constituency.
whyItMatters: If your name is not on this list on election day, you will not be allowed to vote.
whatVoterShouldKnow: Always verify your name on the list a few weeks before the election, even if you have a Voter ID.
beginnerNote: Your Voter ID card (EPIC) is just an ID. The Voter List is the ultimate proof of your right to vote in that election.

title: Election Announcement
simpleExplanation: The formal declaration by the Election Commission stating when elections will be held.
whyItMatters: It sets the entire election machinery into motion and enforces the Model Code of Conduct.
whatVoterShouldKnow: The announcement includes dates for nomination, voting, and counting.
beginnerNote: Once dates are announced, the government cannot launch new schemes that might influence voters.

title: Model Code of Conduct
simpleExplanation: A set of guidelines for political parties and candidates to ensure free and fair elections.
whyItMatters: It prevents the ruling party from misusing official machinery and ensures a level playing field.
whatVoterShouldKnow: Citizens can report violations using the cVIGIL app.
beginnerNote: It is essentially a rulebook for politicians to play fair during the election season.

title: Candidate Nomination
simpleExplanation: The process where individuals officially declare they are running for office.
whyItMatters: It finalizes the list of people you can actually vote for.
whatVoterShouldKnow: Candidates must disclose their assets, criminal records, and educational qualifications.
beginnerNote: You have the right to know the background of every candidate in your area before you vote.

title: Campaigning
simpleExplanation: The period where candidates and parties try to persuade people to vote for them.
whyItMatters: It helps voters understand the promises, policies, and vision of different parties.
whatVoterShouldKnow: Campaigning strictly stops 48 hours before polling begins.
beginnerNote: Listen to all sides, but make your own independent decision.

title: Polling Day
simpleExplanation: The specific day(s) when voters physically go to polling stations to cast their vote.
whyItMatters: This is the core event of democracy where citizens exercise their power.
whatVoterShouldKnow: Mobile phones are not allowed inside the voting compartment.
beginnerNote: Polling day is usually declared a paid holiday for workers so everyone gets a chance to vote.

title: EVM / VVPAT Voting
simpleExplanation: The electronic machinery used to securely record your vote.
whyItMatters: It ensures voting is fast, secret, and accurate.
whatVoterShouldKnow: EVM is the button machine. VVPAT is the glass window that shows a printed slip of your vote for 7 seconds.
beginnerNote: Look at the VVPAT window to visually confirm your vote went to the right candidate.

title: Vote Counting
simpleExplanation: The process of securely opening EVMs and tallying the votes.
whyItMatters: It determines the exact number of votes each candidate received.
whatVoterShouldKnow: Counting is done under strict security and video surveillance in front of candidate representatives.
beginnerNote: The machines are sealed and kept in strong rooms until counting day.

title: Result Declaration
simpleExplanation: The official announcement of who won the election in each constituency.
whyItMatters: It formally finalizes the new representatives for the upcoming term.
whatVoterShouldKnow: The candidate with the most votes wins, even if they don't have an absolute majority.
beginnerNote: You can watch live, official results on the ECI Results portal.

title: Government Formation
simpleExplanation: The party or coalition with the majority of winning seats forms the ruling government.
whyItMatters: This determines who will run the country or state for the next term.
whatVoterShouldKnow: At the national level, 272 seats are needed for a majority in the Lok Sabha.
beginnerNote: Even if your candidate didn't win, the winning candidate is now responsible for representing everyone in your area.
